stem: A healthcare medical diagnosis uses 2 parallel tests for redundancy. The condition detected requires at least one test to function. test 1 has a reliability of 81%. test 2 has a reliability between 72% and 90% (uncertain due to limited operational data). What is the overall probability that the condition detected?

ground_truth.answer: P(condition detected) ranges from 0.9468 to 0.981. Point estimate: 0.9639.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 2 tests fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.19 × 0.28 = 0.0532. P(works) = 0.9468.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.19 × 0.1 = 0.019. P(works) = 0.981.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
